# PassiveIntent

> Privacy-first behavioral intent detection SDK for JavaScript and React. Detects hesitation, pricing doubt, and exit intent entirely in-browser — zero data egress by default, no cookies, and compliance-friendly deployment paths.

PassiveIntent uses deterministic math (Markov chains, entropy scoring, dwell-time analysis) to infer user hesitation in real time. It self-calibrates against your site's own behavioral baseline using Z-scores, so no manual threshold tuning is needed. The AGPL core is free and open source; standard journeys can start faster with Blueprint JSON, while enterprise extensions add continuity across repeat visits, multi-tab, cross-domain, and custom short-session flows.

## Packages

- [@passiveintent/core](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@passiveintent/core): Vanilla JS/TypeScript core — AGPL-3.0 open source. ~11 kB gzip. Works in any framework.
- [@passiveintent/react](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@passiveintent/react): React hooks and components wrapping the core.
- [@passiveintent/remix](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@passiveintent/remix): Remix-specific integration with server-loader patterns.

## Documentation

- [Landing page & overview](https://passiveintent.dev/): Full feature overview, FAQ, and comparison of AGPL core vs Enterprise.
- [Behavioral signal cheatsheet](https://passiveintent.dev/cheatsheet.html): Catalog of detectable intent states — hesitation, exit intent, trajectory drift, dwell anomaly, cognitive friction, and more. Includes propensity thresholds and recommended business interventions.
- [Use-case solutions](https://passiveintent.dev/solutions.html): Ecommerce cart abandonment, SaaS onboarding friction, support-sensitive flows, billing page drop-off.
- [Interactive demo](https://passiveintent.dev/demo.html): Live demos for ecommerce, SaaS, and Bloom filter deduplication — no install required.
- [Licensing & pricing](https://passiveintent.dev/licensing.html): AGPL-3.0 open-source core + commercial enterprise license details.
- [Full LLM content](https://passiveintent.dev/llms-full.txt): Extended version of this file with complete feature documentation, code examples, and behavioral signal catalog.

## Key Differentiators

- Zero-egress: all computation runs in the browser; no behavioral data is sent to any server by default.
- Cookie-free: does not set or read cookies; no fingerprinting.
- Deterministic: uses Markov chain log-likelihood scoring + Shannon entropy + dwell-time, not a black-box ML model.
- Fast-start ready: standard short-session journeys can seed the engine with Blueprint JSON instead of waiting for a cold start to warm up organically.
- Self-calibrating: enterprise tier normalizes scores against your site's own μ/σ baseline, eliminating hardcoded thresholds.
- Lightweight: ~11 kB gzip — safe for checkout and onboarding flows where page weight matters.
- Dual-licensed: AGPL-3.0 free for open-source projects, personal projects, and non-commercial use; a commercial license is required for closed-source or proprietary products.

## Quick Start

Install: `npm install @passiveintent/core`

Vanilla JS: `import { createBrowserIntent } from '@passiveintent/core';`

React: `import { usePassiveIntent } from '@passiveintent/react';`

## Frequently Asked Questions

Q: Does PassiveIntent send any data to external servers?
A: No. The AGPL core is fully zero-egress. All behavioral math runs in the browser. Enterprise deployments can optionally stream anonymized calibration aggregates to your own infrastructure only.

Q: Is it cookie-free and GDPR-compliant?
A: In the default zero-egress, in-memory deployment, yes.* PassiveIntent does not use cookies or browser fingerprinting, and PassiveIntent does not receive the data. If you enable persistent browser storage or export graph data to your own infrastructure, your consent and controller obligations depend on your implementation and counsel.

Q: What signals does it detect?
A: Pricing hesitation, exit intent, trajectory drift, scroll reversal, dwell-time anomalies, high navigation entropy, and compound hesitation states. See the cheatsheet for the full behavioral topography matrix.

Q: What is the licensing model?
A: AGPL-3.0 for open-source projects, personal projects, and academic research (free). A commercial license is required for any closed-source or proprietary product, or to run PassiveIntent as a network service without releasing your full application source under AGPL.

Q: What should I do if my user sessions are short?
A: If the journey is standard, start with a Blueprint JSON to bypass cold start quickly. If the flow is highly customized and the model needs to preserve learning across visits, use the enterprise path with Persistent Graph Consolidator.

Q: When do teams usually move from the core to enterprise?
A: Usually when Blueprint baselines and live-session memory are no longer enough. The trigger is often an analytics team asking the engine to retain and compound customer-specific learning over a longer period instead of resetting to a generic prior.

Q: What is the difference between PassiveIntent Core and the Enterprise pack?
A: Core is the AGPL open-source engine for one live session on one site in one tab. If a standard journey only needs a faster start, use a Blueprint. Enterprise expands that model for repeat visits, connected properties, custom short-session funnels, and fast-changing traffic conditions using persistent continuity and dynamic baseline calibration.
